questions.text: The working area of a computer monitor is sometimes called:
questions.explanation: In multimedia and UI design, the available screen space for displaying content is often referred to as 'real estate'.

questions.text: The type of image used for photo-realistic images and for complex drawings requiring fine detail is the:
questions.explanation: Bitmaps (or raster images) are composed of individual pixels, making them ideal for representing the complex color variations and details found in photographs.

questions.text: The type of image used for lines, boxes, circles, polygons, and other graphic shapes that can be mathematically expressed in angles, coordinates, and distances is the:
questions.explanation: Vector graphics use mathematical formulas to define shapes, allowing them to be scaled infinitely without loss of quality.

questions.text: The picture elements that make up a bitmap are called:
questions.explanation: Pixels (short for picture elements) are the smallest unit of a bitmap image.

questions.text: Which process allows you to smoothly blend two images so that one image seems to melt into another?
questions.explanation: Morphing is a technique used to create a smooth transition from one image to another.

questions.text: The process that computes the bounds of the shapes of colors within a bitmap image and then derives the polygon object that describes that image is called:
questions.explanation: Autotracing converts a bitmap into a vector format by identifying shape boundaries.

questions.text: What is the term for when the computer finally uses intricate algorithms to apply the effects you have specified on the objects you have created for a final 3D image?
questions.explanation: Rendering is the final step in the 3D pipeline where the computer generates the final 2D image based on the 3D data and lighting instructions.

questions.text: Which technology provides a method for viewing a single surrounding image as if you were "inside" the picture and able to look up or down, turn, or zoom in on features?
questions.explanation: QuickTime VR (QTVR) was a common technology for creating panoramic, interactive environments.

questions.text: An annoying flash of strange colors in your image while the computer remakes its color lookup table and the old colors change to the new is known as:
questions.explanation: Palette flashing occurs when a computer updates its color palette, causing a brief visual disruption in colors.

questions.text: The process whereby the color value of each pixel is changed to the closest matching color value in the target palette, using a mathematical algorithm, is called:
questions.explanation: Dithering is used to approximate unavailable colors by placing pixels of available colors close together.

questions.text: The way(s) to start creating your project's interface is/are:

choices.label: A
choices.text: start with pencil, eraser, and paper.

choices.label: B
choices.text: outline your project and graphic ideas.

choices.label: D
choices.text: use 3 x 5 index cards and shuffle them.

questions.explanation: Planning an interface involves various low-fidelity brainstorming and structural methods including sketching, outlining, and storyboarding.

questions.text: Which image file type is the best for photographs?
questions.explanation: Photographs require complex color transitions that are best captured by pixel-based bitmap formats.

questions.text: A 16-bit image is capable of representing how many different colors?
questions.explanation: The number of colors is calculated as \( 2^n \). For 16 bits, \( 2^{16} = 65,536 \).

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a vector-drawn object?
questions.explanation: Photographs are bitmap images; geometric shapes like lines and polygons are standard vector objects.

questions.text: "Unlimited use" of stock photography may actually impose a limitation on:

choices.label: A
choices.text: the number of units you can distribute without paying more.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the number of changes you can make to the image.

choices.label: C
choices.text: converting the image to another file format.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the filters you may use to alter the image.

choices.label: E
choices.text: the price you can charge for your product.

questions.explanation: Standard stock photography licenses often include a cap on the number of printed or distributed copies allowed before an extended license is required.

questions.text: The area of memory where data such as text and images is temporarily stored when you cut or copy them within an application is called the:
questions.explanation: The clipboard is the operating system's temporary storage buffer for data transfers between applications or locations.

questions.text: Perhaps the single most significant advance in computer image processing during the late 1980s was the development of:
questions.explanation: The late 1980s saw the birth of sophisticated image-editing software like Adobe Photoshop, which revolutionized digital image processing.

questions.text: When an image imported on a Macintosh is viewed on a PC, it appears:

choices.label: A
choices.text: darker and richer because the values have changed.

choices.label: B
choices.text: lighter and less saturated because the values have changed.

choices.label: C
choices.text: darker and richer even though the values have not changed.

choices.label: D
choices.text: lighter and less saturated even though the values have not changed.

questions.explanation: Due to differences in default gamma settings (historically 1.8 for Mac and 2.2 for PC), Mac images look darker on PC screens even if the file data is identical.

questions.text: Graphic artists designing for print media use vector-drawn objects because:

choices.label: A
choices.text: they can contain more subtle variations in shading than bitmap graphics.

choices.label: B
choices.text: printing inks respond better to them.

choices.label: C
choices.text: they can be converted across platforms more easily.

choices.label: D
choices.text: they can be printed in any size.

choices.label: E
choices.text: they can be viewed directly in Web browsers.

questions.explanation: Vector objects are resolution-independent, allowing them to be scaled to any size (from a business card to a billboard) without pixelation.

questions.text: The 3D process of extending a plane surface some distance, either perpendicular to the shape's outline or along a defined path, is called:
questions.explanation: Extruding is the process of creating a 3D shape by pushing a 2D profile along a linear or curved path.

questions.text: The VGA standard is:

choices.label: A
choices.text: 8 bits of color information per pixel in a matrix of 640 pixels across and 480 pixels down.

choices.label: B
choices.text: 16 bits of color information per pixel in a matrix of 640 pixels across and 480 pixels down.

choices.label: C
choices.text: 16 bits of color information per pixel in a matrix of 800 pixels across and 600 pixels down.

choices.label: D
choices.text: 24 bits of color information per pixel in a matrix of 800 pixels across and 600 pixels down.

choices.label: E
choices.text: 24 bits of color information per pixel in a matrix of 1024 pixels across and 768 pixels down.

questions.explanation: While original VGA supported 4-bit at 640x480, the commonly cited multimedia baseline for VGA refers to 8-bit (256 colors) at 640x480.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct HTML representation of magenta (red + blue)?
questions.explanation: In hexadecimal (HTML/CSS), colors are represented as #RRGGBB. Magenta is full red (FF), no green (00), and full blue (FF).

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a color specification format?
questions.explanation: GIF is a graphics file format, whereas RGB, HSB, CMYK, and CIE are models or systems for specifying colors.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a native Windows graphics file format?
questions.explanation: PICT is the native graphics format for the Apple Macintosh platform.

questions.text: TIFF stands for:
questions.explanation: TIFF stands for Tagged Image File Format, a flexible, bitmap image format.
